what_it_does: Records metadata for a single image attachment in the design-assets channel, linking an attachment identifier to an image:// target URI and a creation timestamp.
observations: The file consists solely of YAML frontmatter with no body content.
Channel is design-assets and attachment_type is images.
Target URI is image://design-assets/icons/image (20), containing a space and parentheses.
standouts: Target URI uses an image:// scheme with a path containing a space and parentheses.
Attachment is categorized under the design-assets channel with type images.
Record is purely frontmatter metadata with no descriptive body.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured attachment metadata.
Markdown file path follows an att-<id>.md naming pattern under channel_attachments/.
References an external asset via a custom image:// URI scheme.
concerns: Target URI contains unescaped spaces and parentheses, which may cause parsing or linking issues.
No human-readable title, label, alt text, or filename is provided.
Added timestamp is one year in the future, suggesting possible fixture/test data or a clock issue.
notes: A bare attachment ledger entry that points the design-assets channel to an image resource via a custom URI.
questions: What does 'image (20)' identify, and is the target URI resolvable by the system?
Why is the added timestamp set in the future?
Is there any associated message, conversation, or usage context for this attachment?
purpose_vs_actual: n/a (no explicit purpose statement, but the content is consistent with an attachment metadata record)
intention_signals: To register an image asset in the design-assets channel so the channel memory system can reference and retrieve it.
novelty: The image:// URI scheme and a target path that includes a space and parentheses are somewhat unusual for a stored attachment reference.
contribution: Provides a dated, typed, URI-based reference for an image attachment in the design-assets channel, supporting lookup and linking within channel memory.
summary_for_embedding: A channel-memory attachment metadata file that registers an image asset in the design-assets channel via an image://design-assets/icons/image (20) URI with a 2026 timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To record and make discoverable an image attachment belonging to the design-assets channel.
